probes.evidence_summary: Solver reports converged=False with 0 NR iterations; 83% of buses remain at flat start. Original PASS classification is not supported.

probes.claim: PyPSA stochastic optimization documentation vs functionality discrepancy
probes.evidence_summary: D-2 correctly states stochastic API is documented; A-8 correctly concludes FAIL due to optimizer integration bugs. Both findings are valid -- documentation exists but functionality does not work.

probes.claim: pandapower stochastic DCOPF 2.1% convergence rate on ACTIVSg2000
probes.evidence_summary: Probe confirms extremely low convergence rate (0.42% vs claimed 2.1%) for PYPOWER interior point on perturbed ACTIVSg2000. Uniform load scaling alone converges reliably.

probes.claim: Uniform LMPs across all 10,000 buses on MEDIUM DCOPF
probes.evidence_summary: LMPs are truly uniform (std=6e-12); no branch exceeds 85% loading. Artificial congestion produces LMP spread (16.3-24.4 $/MWh), confirming base case simply has no binding constraints.

probes.claim: pandapower PTDF flow divergence from DCPF on MEDIUM (7.43 pu)
probes.evidence_summary: Probe reproduces exact max diff of 7.43 pu on ACTIVSg10k; case39 matches perfectly (0.0 diff). Error is real and scale-dependent.

probes.claim: pandapower lambda values of 1e25 with in_service=False
probes.evidence_summary: No lambda values of 1e25 observed; in_service=False either converges with normal lambdas or throws exception. Convergence pattern identical to max_p_mw=0 approach.

probes.claim: pandapower PTDF error attribution to shunt elements
probes.evidence_summary: 7.43 pu error reproduced; caused by missing Pbusinj/Pfinj correction terms from phase shifters, not shunt elements (which have zero MW). Applying correction eliminates ALL error to machine precision.

probes.claim: GridCal PTDF flow predictions diverge from DCPF by up to 743 MW on MEDIUM
probes.evidence_summary: Reproduced max diff of 743.46 MW (LA vs DCPF) and 15,139.36 MW (PTDF@Sbus vs DCPF), matching original claim exactly. Same root cause as pandapower (phase-shifter correction).

probes.claim: PowerModels C-5/C-8 projected infeasibility on ACTIVSg10k
probes.evidence_summary: BFS scope sizes bracket claimed range; per-contingency DCPF time matches claims; SCOPF with 5 contingencies timed out at 567s confirming infeasibility at 500.

probes.claim: PowerSimulations C-3 DCOPF scale timing was estimated, not measured
probes.evidence_summary: C-3 reported wall_clock_seconds=null with estimated <60s; probe confirmed no actual timing was done. First run timed out at 600s (includes JIT).

probes.claim: PowerSimulations dispatch values ~100x larger than component limits
probes.evidence_summary: Dispatch values from read_variables() are in MW, not pu; they match Pmax when compared in the same units. The A-4 evaluation mislabeled MW values as pu, creating a false appearance of ~100x mismatch.

probes.claim: PowerSimulations custom constraint binding dual extraction
probes.evidence_summary: Binding flow gate constraint produces non-zero dual (-0.1398); dual extraction via JuMP.dual() works for both binding and non-binding cases.

probes.claim: PowerSimulations distributed slack qualified_pass on uncongested network
probes.evidence_summary: PTDF is structurally distributed-slack (0 angle vars); but on uncongested IEEE 39-bus, both PTDF and DCP formulations produce identical dispatch (max diff 1.5e-7), making the distinction a no-op.

probes.claim: PowerSimulations ACPF scale failure diagnostics on ACTIVSg10k
probes.evidence_summary: ACPF on 10k-bus network returns Missing (silent failure) in 5.5s with zero diagnostic info; no iteration count, no residual, no configurable tolerances.
Codecov badge shows 78% coverage, not 100% as reported in E-6; the evaluation misread or cited a stale badge.

probes.claim: MATPOWER distributed slack DC OPF on MEDIUM takes 66 minutes via opt_model/MIPS
probes.evidence_summary: MIPS solve is confirmed extremely slow (141s for 1 iteration); 65-min total for convergence is plausible given the per-iteration cost. Dense PTDF matrix is 400x slower than sparse formulation.

probes.claim: MATPOWER contingency sweep on MEDIUM has 97% time in Octave containers.Map overhead
probes.evidence_summary: containers.Map overhead confirmed as dominant bottleneck; LODF screening of 12,706 N-1 cases completes in 0.81s while Map adjacency build times out at 300s.

probes.claim: MATPOWER MOST loadmd() fails at data ingestion on ACTIVSg2000
probes.evidence_summary: loadmd() confirmed to fail with exact error message about non-consecutive bus numbering on ACTIVSg 2000; ext2int resolves bus issue but reveals further MOST input requirements.
